author: 0xrubo
content: that's my understanding as well. i believe that's what we have now, and what these standards work is trying to cover. so, if you wanna use some stateless exec client, you to take the block, witness, and check that guest exec client requirements for data serialization, implement them yourself, and pass it. unless exec client has a small tool doing that for you.

author: 0xrubo
content: for now, yes. and we're adding our own simple format to the existing ones. i'll share the details by monday with you and <@427491045308235776>

author: 0xrubo
content: i presume that for big mainnet blocks, both rlp and ssz are too heavy. we may need a lighter format, given zkvms are not fast. but we can partially go with rlp, as rpc interface is able to return blocks in rlp, making it very convenient to pass them that way
